basics.description: Deploy the **AI Foundry Landing Zone Accelerator** (FinOps Edition). Pick a blueprint, optionally wire to an existing ALZ hub, and review before deploy. Reference architecture: [github.com/kimvaddi/ai-foundry-landing-zone-accelerator](https://github.com/kimvaddi/ai-foundry-landing-zone-accelerator).

validations.message: Owner or Contributor on the subscription is required (this template creates resource groups).

location.label: Primary region
location.toolTip: Region for the foundation (LAW, App Insights, Key Vault) and Foundry account. AI Search location can be overridden in the next step.

basics.name: workload
basics.label: Workload short name
basics.toolTip: 3-8 lower-case chars. Used in every resource name (e.g. log-<workload>-<env>-<suffix>).

constraints.validationMessage: Lower-case letters/digits, 3-8 chars, starts with a letter.

basics.name: env
basics.label: Environment
basics.toolTip: Environment label baked into resource names and the `env` tag.

basics.name: costCenter
basics.label: Cost center tag
basics.toolTip: Stamped onto the `costCenter` resource tag. The FinOps showback workbook groups spend by this tag.

options.text: A blueprint pre-fills sane defaults for compute, APIM, and safety toggles. You can override any of them in the later steps.

elements.name: blueprintPicker
elements.label: Blueprint
elements.toolTip: Pick a starting profile. See the README cost-guidance table for monthly cost estimates per blueprint.

allowedValues.label: poc-standalone-spoke (~$70/mo — adds spoke VNet + 21 PDNS + PEs)
poc-hub-connected (~$45/mo — peers to your hub, reuses central PDNS)

elements.name: networkMode
elements.label: Network mode
elements.toolTip: standalone = the module owns the spoke VNet, 21 private DNS zones, NSGs, route tables. hub-connected = peer to an existing ALZ hub, reuse the hub's central PDNS zones.

allowedValues.label: standalone (greenfield) — module creates the spoke VNet + DNS
hub-connected (brownfield) — peer to existing ALZ hub

elements.name: vnetAddressSpace
elements.label: Spoke VNet address space
elements.toolTip: /20 fits all 9 subnets with room. Must not overlap with your hub's address space.

elements.name: searchLocation
elements.label: AI Search region (override)
elements.toolTip: Leave blank to co-locate with the primary region. Override only if you turn `enforceApimChokepoint` off (cross-region PE not allowed).

options.text: Hub-connected mode requires an existing ALZ-style hub. You MUST supply the hub VNet resource ID and the resource IDs of the central private DNS zones the spoke will link to.

elements.name: hubVnetResourceId
elements.label: Hub VNet resource ID
elements.toolTip: Paste the full ARM resource ID of your existing hub VNet. Format: /subscriptions/<SUB-GUID>/resourceGroups/<RG>/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/<VNET-NAME>. Example: /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/hub-rg/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/hub-vnet. Find this in the Portal: open your hub VNet -> Settings -> Properties -> Resource ID.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a full ARM resource ID. Expected format: /subscriptions/<SUB-GUID>/resourceGroups/<RG>/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/<VNET-NAME> (e.g. /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/hub-rg/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/hub-vnet).

elements.name: hubFirewallPrivateIp
elements.label: Hub Azure Firewall private IP
elements.toolTip: Required when forced tunneling is on. Becomes the next-hop in the 0.0.0.0/0 UDR.

constraints.validationMessage: Leave blank or enter a valid IPv4 address.

elements.name: enableForcedTunneling
elements.label: Enable forced tunneling (0/0 → hub firewall)
elements.toolTip: Day-0 recommendation: leave OFF, validate peering + DNS, then flip ON once hub firewall rules are in place.

elements.name: createReverseHubPeer
elements.label: Create reverse hub → spoke peering
elements.toolTip: Only works for same-subscription hubs. Requires write permission on the hub resource group.

options.text: Paste the resource IDs of the central private DNS zones in your hub. The spoke will link its private endpoints to these zones (instead of creating new ones). Each resource ID looks like: /subscriptions/<SUB-GUID>/resourceGroups/<HUB-RG>/providers/Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/<zone-name>. Find each one in the Portal: open the private DNS zone -> Settings -> Properties -> Resource ID. Leave any zone blank if your hub does not host it.

elements.name: pdnsVaultcore
elements.label: privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net resource ID
elements.toolTip: Example: /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/hub-rg/providers/Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net

elements.name: pdnsOpenai
elements.label: privatelink.openai.azure.com resource ID
elements.toolTip: Example: /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/hub-rg/providers/Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/privatelink.openai.azure.com

elements.name: pdnsCognitive
elements.label: privatelink.cognitiveservices.azure.com resource ID
elements.toolTip: Example: /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/hub-rg/providers/Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/privatelink.cognitiveservices.azure.com

elements.name: pdnsSearch
elements.label: privatelink.search.windows.net resource ID
elements.toolTip: Example: /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/hub-rg/providers/Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/privatelink.search.windows.net

elements.name: pdnsBlob
elements.label: privatelink.blob.core.windows.net resource ID
elements.toolTip: Example: /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/hub-rg/providers/Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/privatelink.blob.core.windows.net

elements.name: pdnsApim
elements.label: privatelink.azure-api.net resource ID (APIM)
elements.toolTip: Only required if you deploy APIM in internal mode. Example: /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/hub-rg/providers/Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/privatelink.azure-api.net

elements.name: pdnsCosmos
elements.label: privatelink.documents.azure.com resource ID (Cosmos)
elements.toolTip: Only required if a Foundry project enables Agent Service capability host. Example: /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/hub-rg/providers/Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones/privatelink.documents.azure.com

options.text: Defaults below reflect the selected blueprint. Toggle to override. AI Search is always on.

elements.name: apimDeploy
elements.label: Deploy APIM AI Gateway
elements.toolTip: Provisions an APIM StandardV2 instance with token-limit, semantic-cache, content-safety, and emit-token-metric policies. Required for the chokepoint pattern.

elements.name: enforceApimChokepoint
elements.label: Enforce APIM as the SINGLE chokepoint
elements.toolTip: Sets Foundry + Search publicNetworkAccess=Disabled, attaches a Search PE, and locks NSGs so ONLY the APIM subnet can reach the PE subnet. Recommended enterprise default. Requires APIM in internal/external mode.

elements.name: bastionDeploy
elements.label: Deploy Azure Bastion
elements.toolTip: Standard SKU Bastion in the spoke. Required to RDP/SSH into the jump or build VMs.

options.text: Each VM you enable below will reveal a matching field on the Compute Credentials step -- Jump VM (Windows) asks for an admin password, Build VM (Linux) asks for an OpenSSH public key. Uncheck any VM you don't plan to log into and the matching credential field disappears.

elements.name: jumpvmDeploy
elements.label: Deploy Windows jump VM
elements.toolTip: Standard_B2s Windows VM in the JumpboxSubnet. Reached via Bastion. Checking this will ask for an admin password on the Compute Credentials step.

elements.name: buildvmDeploy
elements.label: Deploy Linux build VM (CI runner)
elements.toolTip: Standard_B2s Linux VM in the BuildAgentSubnet. Checking this will ask for an OpenSSH public key on the Compute Credentials step (Linux Azure VMs have password auth disabled by policy).

elements.name: appGatewayDeploy
elements.label: Deploy Application Gateway (WAF_v2)
elements.toolTip: WAF v2 in front of APIM (or your app). Public IP + managed cert.

elements.name: caeDeploy
elements.label: Deploy Container Apps Environment
elements.toolTip: Internal CAE in the ContainerAppEnvironmentSubnet. Hosts agent runtimes.

elements.name: notificationsDeploy
elements.label: Deploy Teams notifications (Logic App)
elements.toolTip: Logic App that posts an Adaptive Card to a Teams channel via Workflows Incoming Webhook. Ships disabled.

elements.name: enableFoundryAgentInjection
elements.label: Enable Foundry Agent Service injection
elements.toolTip: Registers the AIFoundrySubnet on the Foundry account so the Standard Agent Service can inject. Requires AKS GPU SKU capacity in the chosen region.

elements.name: createFoundryCapabilityHost
elements.label: Create Foundry account-level capability host (Agents)
elements.toolTip: Required for the Standard Agent Service to host agents. Implies agent injection.

options.text: These secrets are passed via Secure Strings — never logged. They are required when the corresponding VM is deployed.

elements.name: jumpvmAdminPassword
elements.toolTip: Local admin password for the Windows jump VM. Min 12 chars, 3 of 4 categories (upper, lower, digit, symbol).

constraints.validationMessage: Min 12 chars, must include 3 of: upper, lower, digit, symbol.

elements.name: buildvmSshPublicKey
elements.label: Build VM SSH public key
elements.toolTip: OpenSSH single-line format. Generate with: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C build-vm

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid OpenSSH public key line (ssh-rsa / ssh-ed25519 / ecdsa-sha2-*).

options.text: APIM publisher details + the AI-Gateway safety/cache policies that get applied to the foundry-default product.

elements.name: apimProductTokensPerMinute
elements.label: Token rate limit (TPM) on foundry-default product
elements.toolTip: Templated into product-token-limit.xml. Above this, APIM returns 429.

elements.name: enableSemanticCache
elements.label: Enable APIM semantic cache (provisions Azure Managed Redis)
elements.toolTip: Provisions Azure Managed Redis (Balanced_B0) + APIM external cache + embeddings backend. ~60-80% cost savings on repeat prompts.

options.text: Semantic cache needs an embedding model. Microsoft's 2025 guidance: text-embedding-3-small is the recommended default for cache lookup (faster, ~1x cost, sufficient accuracy). Use text-embedding-3-large only when you need higher recall on subtle prompt similarity (~5x cost, doubles Redis vector storage). text-embedding-ada-002 is legacy and kept for back-compat. The picked model is auto-added to the Models & Projects JSON on the next tab.

elements.name: embeddingsModel
elements.label: Embedding model for semantic cache
elements.toolTip: Picked model is auto-included in the Model deployments JSON on the next tab.

allowedValues.label: text-embedding-3-large (Higher recall, ~5x cost, doubles Redis vector storage)
text-embedding-ada-002 (Legacy — older generation, kept for back-compat)
Custom (you manage the Models JSON entry yourself)

elements.name: embeddingsCustomName
elements.label: Custom embedding deployment name
elements.toolTip: Must match the 'name' field of an entry you add yourself to the Model deployments JSON on the next tab.

constraints.validationMessage: Letters, digits, hyphens; must start with a letter; 2-64 chars.

options.text: Edit the rows below. Defaults reflect the blueprint you picked on tab 1. Add or delete rows with the toolbar. If you need a model that isn't in the dropdown, use the Bicep or Terraform stack directly.

APIM semantic cache is enabled, so the embedding model picked on the APIM tab has been added as a row in the Model deployments grid below. The APIM gateway will fail to start the cache if that row's 'Deployment name' is renamed or deleted. Keep the row, or pick 'Custom' on the APIM tab and manage the row yourself.

elements.name: modelDeployments
elements.ariaLabel: Edit Azure OpenAI / Foundry model deployments
elements.label: Model deployments
elements.defaultValue: [parse(concat('[', if(startsWith(steps('blueprint').blueprintPicker, 'prod-'), '{"name":"gpt-4o","modelName":"gpt-4o","modelVersion":"2024-11-20","skuName":"GlobalStandard","capacity":"50"}', '{"name":"gpt-4o-mini","modelName":"gpt-4o-mini","modelVersion":"2024-07-18","skuName":"GlobalStandard","capacity":"10"}'), if(and(coalesce(steps('components').apimDeploy, false), coalesce(steps('apim').enableSemanticCache, false), not(equals(coalesce(steps('apim').embeddingsModel, ''), '_custom'))), concat(',', if(equals(steps('apim').embeddingsModel, 'text-embedding-3-small'), '{"name":"text-embedding-3-small","modelName":"text-embedding-3-small","modelVersion":"1","skuName":"Standard","capacity":"10"}', if(equals(steps('apim').embeddingsModel, 'text-embedding-3-large'), '{"name":"text-embedding-3-large","modelName":"text-embedding-3-large","modelVersion":"1","skuName":"Standard","capacity":"10"}', if(equals(steps('apim').embeddingsModel, 'text-embedding-ada-002'), '{"name":"text-embedding-ada-002","modelName":"text-embedding-ada-002","modelVersion":"2","skuName":"Standard","capacity":"10"}', '')))), ''), ']'))]

validations.message: Letters, digits, '.', '_', '-'; must start with a letter; 2-64 chars.

elements.name: foundryProjects
elements.label: Foundry projects
elements.defaultValue: [parse(if(startsWith(steps('blueprint').blueprintPicker, 'prod-'), '[{"name":"platform","displayName":"Platform team","description":"Foundry workspace for the central AI platform team."},{"name":"business-unit-a","displayName":"Business unit A","description":"Isolated workspace for BU-A apps and agents."}]', '[{"name":"default","displayName":"Default project","description":"Foundry project."}]'))]

validations.message: Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens; must start with a letter; 2-32 chars.

element.placeholder: Short description (required so the JSON always has the key).

options.text: Optional — leave blank to skip. The Logic App always ships in state=Disabled; you can flip it on after wiring the webhook.

elements.name: enableNotificationsLogicApp
elements.label: Set Logic App state to Enabled on deploy

elements.name: teamsWebhookUrl
elements.toolTip: From Teams channel → Workflows → 'Post to a channel when a webhook request is received'. SecureString — never logged.

constraints.validationMessage: Leave blank or paste a valid Teams workflow webhook URL.

elements.name: notificationEmails
elements.label: Fallback notification emails (comma-separated)
elements.toolTip: Optional. e.g. ai-platform@contoso.com,secops@contoso.com

options.text: Implements Microsoft's recommended Foundry RBAC model (learn.microsoft.com/azure/ai-foundry/concepts/rbac-azure-ai-foundry). Default = OFF. Toggle on to assign Foundry Owner / Project Manager / User to Entra groups, plus Foundry MI → Search Index Data Reader and VM MIs → Key Vault Secrets User. Every field is optional — leave blank to skip an individual assignment. All assignments use role GUIDs (rename-safe).

elements.name: enablePostDeployRbac
elements.label: Enable post-deploy RBAC assignments
elements.toolTip: Master switch. When OFF, no role assignments are emitted regardless of the object IDs below.

options.text: Find object IDs in Entra: Groups → <group> → Object ID, or SPN → Enterprise Applications → <app> → Object ID. CLI: az ad group show --group <name> --query id -o tsv  •  az ad sp show --id <appId> --query id -o tsv

elements.name: foundryAdminGroupObjectId
elements.label: Foundry Owner group (object ID)
elements.toolTip: Entra group that gets Foundry Owner on the Foundry account. Highly privileged — full project + model + RBAC management. Example: 11111111-2222-3333-4444-555555555555

constraints.validationMessage: Leave blank or paste a valid Entra group object ID (GUID).

elements.name: foundryLeadGroupObjectId
elements.label: Foundry Project Manager group (object ID)
elements.toolTip: Entra group that gets Foundry Project Manager — team leads who publish agents, create projects, and assign Foundry User to others.

constraints.validationMessage: Leave blank or paste a valid Entra group object ID (GUID).

elements.name: foundryDeveloperGroupObjectId
elements.label: Foundry User group (object ID)
elements.toolTip: Entra group that gets Foundry User — developers who build agents and call models. Least-privilege Foundry role.

constraints.validationMessage: Leave blank or paste a valid Entra group object ID (GUID).

elements.name: platformReaderGroupObjectId
elements.label: Reader group (object ID)
elements.toolTip: Entra group that gets Reader on the Foundry account AND on the platform resource group — auditors, SREs, anyone who needs read-only visibility.

constraints.validationMessage: Leave blank or paste a valid Entra group object ID (GUID).

elements.name: deploymentSpnObjectId
elements.label: CI/CD deployment SPN (object ID)
elements.toolTip: Service principal object ID that gets Contributor on the platform resource group — your GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps deployer.

constraints.validationMessage: Leave blank or paste a valid SPN object ID (GUID).
